highlights: Hippieus is a decentralized storage platform on the Bittensor network that functions as a high-speed, cost-effective alternative to Amazon S3 by using incentive mechanisms to reward miners for uptime and performance.

The "Flat White or F**k Off" coffee brand illustrates the "tyranny of choice" principle, showing that limiting a menu to a single high-quality product can reduce service times to under 20 seconds while driving viral brand engagement.

Modern entrepreneurs are increasingly using "viral validation" to test business concepts on social media before investing in physical infrastructure, leveraging platforms like TikTok to build an audience before launch.

Current digital media trends show that many online influencers operate as "slaves to the algorithm," adopting exaggerated personas and controversial stances primarily to trigger engagement and drive subscription revenue.
summary:
This episode of *This Week in Startups* explores the intersection of decentralized technology, viral marketing, and modern media culture. The first half of the show features an interview with the founders of Hippieus, a decentralized cloud storage platform operating as a subnet on the Bittensor (TAO) network. The founders explain how they are leveraging blockchain incentives to create a high-speed, reliable alternative to Amazon’s S3. Unlike traditional crypto mining that solves abstract puzzles, Hippieus rewards "miners" for providing actual storage utility, using a protocol called Arion to ensure performance and redundancy. This segment highlights a significant trend toward decentralized physical infrastructure (DePIN) and the shift of crypto compute toward productive, real-world applications like AI and data storage.

The second segment focuses on "Flat White or F-off," a viral coffee brand inspired by a joke from marketing expert Rory Sutherland. The founders describe how they used "lean startup" principles and "pattern disruption" to transform a viral social media concept into a physical pop-up business that eliminates the "tyranny of choice" by serving only one type of drink. By telling customers to "f-off" if they wanted customizations, the brand gained massive traction on TikTok and Instagram, illustrating how modern entrepreneurs can validate business ideas through digital attention before seeking capital. Host Jason Calacanis expressed enough interest in the project’s cultural momentum to offer a potential investment through a syndicate.

The episode concludes with an "Off Duty" discussion on the 2024 Oscars and the evolution of media consumption. The hosts debate the merits of Best Picture nominees and the cultural relevance of traditional cinema compared to declining art forms like opera and ballet. They also touch on the influence of the "manosphere" and the "slaves to the algorithm" in content creation, referencing a Louis Theroux documentary. Throughout the show, a recurring theme is the power of incentives—whether they are driving decentralized server networks, viral marketing stunts, or the performance-based nature of modern social media influencers.
description:
Legendary British ad man Rory Sutherland invented a fake business on a podcast, a coffee shop appealing to the busy commuter called “Flat White or F**k Off.”

But it’s one thing to have a post go big on TikTok or Instagram… but to turn that viral moment into an actual COMPANY is a very different challenge.
On TWiST, Jason and Lon chat with Charlie Hurst, Tom Noble, and Will Sudlow, founders of the online brand “Flat White or F**k Off,” about how they took a funny little slogan and turned it into a string of popular social media accounts, a line of merch, a pop-up café, and let’s face it, an entire lifestyle.

PLUS we’ve got Mog and Dubs from Subnet 75 — Hippius! Hear how they’re using the blockchain for decentralized storage, and providing server space much cheaper than their famous hyperscaler rivals.

youtube_metadata.title: This Decentralized Cloud is Thousands of Times Cheaper Than AWS | EP 2262
youtube_metadata.description:
Legendary British ad man Rory Sutherland invented a fake business on a podcast, a coffee shop appealing to the busy commuter called “Flat White or F**k Off.”

But it’s one thing to have a post go big on TikTok or Instagram… but to turn that viral moment into an actual COMPANY is a very different challenge.
On TWiST, Jason and Lon chat with Charlie Hurst, Tom Noble, and Will Sudlow, founders of the online brand “Flat White or F**k Off,” about how they took a funny little slogan and turned it into a string of popular social media accounts, a line of merch, a pop-up café, and let’s face it, an entire lifestyle.

PLUS we’ve got Mog and Dubs from Subnet 75 — Hippius! Hear how they’re using the blockchain for decentralized storage, and providing server space much cheaper than their famous hyperscaler rivals.
